behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "inside a subdirectory of the watched root". This failure boundary is real recursive filesystem watching, which depends on OS watcher behavior and real directory events. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: an Integration test that injects synthetic events can pass even if recursive watch registration is broken on the actual OS/filesystem. Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM decision and no multi-agent pipeline are involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temp input/output directory. Create `input/nested/a.txt` after startup and verify exactly one transformed output is produced for that file path.

behaviors.description: Deduplication discards a second processing request for the same path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "deduplication logic" and "same file path within a configurable time window". This is core state/time logic that can fail within one object/function boundary without I/O. Rejected higher level (Integration): behavior text does not require OS events, watcher semantics, or module handoff to trigger the bug; direct calls with controlled time are sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the deduper with a known window and fake/controlled clock. Assert first request for path is accepted, second within window is rejected, and a later request after window expiry is accepted.

behaviors.description: Multiple filesystem events for one write result in exactly one processing run.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "operating system emits multiple filesystem events". This explicitly requires real OS event behavior; mocked event streams can hide duplicate-event patterns from real watchers/filesystems. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Integration with synthetic duplicates may not reproduce platform-specific burst ordering/coalescing that causes real double-processing bugs. Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real watched directory, perform a single real file write known to emit multiple watch events (create+change). Assert processing count for that file is exactly one.

behaviors.description: After watched directory deletion and recreation, daemon resumes watching without restart.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running". This is inode/path lifecycle behavior at the OS watcher boundary. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: in-process fakes usually preserve subscription continuity and can miss real watcher invalidation after delete/recreate. Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM decision and no agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory, delete the watched directory, recreate it at same path, then add a new file. Verify the new file is processed without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: Simultaneous arrivals are processed in creation-timestamp order.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "order determined by their creation timestamps" for "multiple files arrive at the same time". Correctness depends on real filesystem timestamp semantics and real arrival/event timing, not just pure sort logic. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Integration tests with mocked metadata can pass while production fails due to filesystem timestamp precision/tie behavior or event ordering. Rejected higher levels: no model output quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon with real filesystem input. Create files with intentionally different creation times, then make them arrive nearly simultaneously (e.g., move/rename into watched dir together). Assert processing order follows filesystem creation timestamp order, not raw event arrival order.

behaviors.description: A file still being written is not processed until writing completes.
behaviors.justification: Forcing phrase: "still being written to when the arrival event fires". This is a real filesystem timing/flush boundary; mocked writes hide partial-write race conditions. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Integration with instant in-memory writes cannot reproduce chunked writes and close/flush timing that trigger premature pickup. Rejected higher levels: no AI/LLM dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real watched directory and write a file in multiple delayed chunks via stream/file descriptor. Assert daemon does not process after first arrival event/chunk, and processes only after final close/complete write.
